title: Coptosapelta macrophylla
taxonomy: Coptosapelta macrophylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Coptosapelta and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. At the kingdom level, Coptosapelta macrophylla is classified as a member of the Plantae kingdom. It is also classified as a type of shrub or small tree in the botanical hierarchy.
uses: Coptosapelta macrophylla, also known as the giant stiltroot, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. Its leaves and roots are used in local remedies to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Additionally, Coptosapelta macrophylla is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Coptosapelta macrophylla for medicinal purposes.
